already_cited: Fan et al. (2025) - Beware of metacognitive laziness
Crosthwaite & Sun (2025) - GenAI and L2 Written Feedback Studies: A Scoping Review
Yu & Xie (2025) - Generative AI vs. Teachers: Feedback Quality, Feedback Uptake, and Revision
Jacobsen et al. (2025) - AI, Expert or Peer? Impact of Perceived Feedback Source
Lo et al. (2025) - Evaluating Teacher, AI, and Hybrid Feedback in English Language Learning (Hong Kong)
Kaliisa et al. (2025) - How does AI compare to human feedback? A meta-analysis
Bauer et al. (2025) - Looking beyond the hype: Understanding the effects of AI on learning
Darvishi et al. (2023) - Impact of AI assistance on student agency
Hansen et al. (2025) - Effects of AI feedback coach on peer feedback quality

new_papers_high_value.title: Breaking human dominance: Investigating learners' preferences for learning feedback from generative AI and human tutors
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Students initially preferred human tutors, but free-dialogue ChatGPT interface increased AI preference; structured AI tools reinforced human tutor preference.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Directly supports algorithm aversion theory and preference-reality gap argument in the proposal; interface design matters for AI feedback acceptance.

new_papers_high_value.title: How GenAI Affects Metacognition in Self-Regulated Learning: Between Enhancement and Inhibition
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: GenAI can enhance metacognitive capabilities (planning, monitoring, evaluation) but also risks metacognitive laziness through over-reliance on AI tools, reducing cognitive engagement.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Core theoretical framing for metacognition component of Study 4 training and the metacognitive laziness hypothesis in the proposal.

new_papers_high_value.title: Hybrid intelligence: Human–AI coevolution and learning
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Hybrid intelligence paradigm positions metacognition as central to effective human-AI interaction; overreliance on AI may decrease human decision-making if students do not exercise metacognitive monitoring.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Provides theoretical framework for hybrid feedback and situates metacognition as the key mechanism in human-AI writing feedback partnerships.

new_papers_high_value.title: AI Tools in Society: Impacts on Cognitive Offloading and the Future of Critical Thinking
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Significant negative correlation between frequent AI tool usage and critical thinking abilities, mediated by increased cognitive offloading.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Evidence for the cognitive offloading mechanism underlying metacognitive laziness; supports the theoretical gating in the proposal's training intervention logic.

new_papers_high_value.title: Factors influencing critical thinking during AI use among university students: the mediating effects of epistemic laziness and metacognitive weakness
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Epistemic laziness and metacognitive weakness mediate the relationship between AI use and critical thinking among university students.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Introduces epistemic laziness as a distinct mechanism from metacognitive weakness; supports multi-faceted training design in Study 4.

new_papers_high_value.title: A Taxonomy of Errors in English as she is spoke: Toward an AI-Based Method of Error Analysis for EFL Writing Instruction
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Developed an LLM-based AI error analysis system using a linguistic taxonomy to identify, categorize, and correct EFL writing errors.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Direct methodological precedent for classification of feedback types and error categories in the proposal's Study 2 classifier.

new_papers_high_value.title: eRevise+RF: A Writing Evaluation System for Assessing Student Essay Revisions and Providing Formative Feedback
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: AWE system deployed with 406 students effectively assessed argumentative essay revisions and determined revision success in responding to feedback.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Exemplar of an operationalized uptake taxonomy system; directly informs design of feedback uptake coding in Study 3.

new_papers_high_value.title: IELTS Writing Revision Platform with Automated Essay Scoring and Adaptive Feedback
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Transformer-based AES with adaptive feedback showed statistically significant score improvements (d=0.504), though effectiveness varied by revision strategy.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Provides effect size benchmark for revision quality improvement from AES in L2 writing; relevant to Study 1 accuracy mapping.

new_papers_high_value.title: Mediating L2 writing revision: The impact of teacher-moderated ChatGPT feedback on L2 accuracy and lexical diversity
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Teacher-moderated ChatGPT feedback impacted L2 writing accuracy and lexical diversity during revision.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Direct empirical evidence for hybrid (teacher-moderated) ChatGPT feedback in L2 writing; supports Study 1's hybrid feedback condition.

new_papers_high_value.title: Learner interaction with, and response to, AI-programmed automated writing evaluation feedback in EFL writing: An exploratory study
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: EFL students' early interactions with AWE focused on error correction; uptake of feedback with linguistic resources was much lower than error corrective feedback.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Directly informs feedback uptake taxonomy design by showing differential uptake rates across feedback types.

new_papers_high_value.title: Leveraging generative AI to foster metacognition and self-directed learning
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: GenAI model used for metacognitive coaching significantly improved students' metacognitive awareness and self-directed learning readiness.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Direct empirical precedent for GenAI-facilitated metacognitive training; supports feasibility of Study 4's training design.

new_papers_high_value.title: DeBiasMe: De-biasing Human-AI Interactions with Metacognitive AIED Interventions
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Advocates for metacognitive AI literacy interventions with deliberate friction to help students critically engage with AI and address cognitive biases.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Design framework for metacognitive AI literacy interventions with 'deliberate friction' — directly applicable to Study 4 training activities.

new_papers_high_value.title: The effect of generative artificial intelligence literacy on academic achievement: the mediating role of academic self-efficacy and the moderating role of critical thinking
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: GenAI literacy enhances academic achievement via academic self-efficacy, with critical thinking moderating this relationship.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Establishes GenAI literacy as a measurable construct with known mechanisms; supports the AI literacy component of the proposal.

new_papers_high_value.title: Leveraging Hybrid Intelligence: How Teacher Agency Influences Behavioural Engagement in Human-AI Collaborative Writing Feedback Practices
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Teacher agency significantly influences behavioural engagement in human-AI collaborative writing feedback practices.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Addresses teacher role in hybrid feedback — currently a gap in the proposal; supports the teacher-AI collaboration framing.

new_papers_high_value.title: Teacher-AI Collaboration in Formative Writing Assessment: Hybrid Feedback Models with Grammarly in ELT
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Explored hybrid feedback models combining teacher and Grammarly AI for formative writing assessment in ELT.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Case study of teacher-AI hybrid feedback in writing assessment; practical model for Study 1 hybrid feedback condition.

new_papers_high_value.title: Comparing teacher E-feedback, AI feedback, and hybrid feedback in enhancing EFL writing skills
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Hybrid (teacher+AI) feedback showed most substantial improvements in EFL writing — task achievement, coherence, and grammatical accuracy.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Empirical evidence for hybrid > AI-only > teacher-only in EFL writing; directly supports the hybrid condition rationale in Study 1.

new_papers_high_value.title: Exploring the Reliability and Validity of AI Scoring in the Continuation Writing Task
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: GPT-4 showed high self-consistency but more lenient scoring than human raters, prioritizing narrative coherence while teachers emphasized linguistic accuracy.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Directly relevant to AI-human scoring divergence (Study 1); supports need for hybrid feedback and LLM-as-rater analysis.

new_papers_high_value.title: Investigating the quality and quantity of feedback enactment in academic writing: Comparing AWE with generative AI
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Students prioritized surface-level compliance with AWE while GenAI users showed different enactment patterns; feedback literacy improved over time with no significant between-group differences.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Directly compares AWE vs GenAI feedback enactment patterns; feedback literacy improvement measured — core to proposal's uptake and literacy focus.

new_papers_high_value.title: Student perceptions of hybrid feedback: Using Gen-AI to enhance engagement with EAP writing feedback
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Integrating Gen-AI was perceived as effective by students with improvements in cognitive, behavioral, and affective engagement with EAP writing feedback.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Measures engagement dimensions of hybrid feedback in EAP — directly relevant to engagement analysis in Studies 3 and 4.

new_papers_high_value.title: EXPLORING TEACHERS' DISPOSITIONS TOWARD AI FEEDBACK ACCURACY AND THE REFINEMENT PROCESS AND THEIR ENHANCEMENT PRACTICES IN EFL WRITING
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: AI frequently overlooked meaning-level problems and focused on surface-level errors; teachers needed to act as feedback curators refining AI-generated feedback.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Empirical evidence for AI feedback accuracy limitations at meaning level — supports need for feedback quality classification in Study 2.

new_papers_high_value.title: The Application of Artificial Intelligence Automated Evaluation Systems in English Writing Instruction: Scoring Accuracy, Feedback Effectiveness, and User Experience
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: AI systems perform well on linguistic form evaluation (grammar, vocabulary) but remain inadequate in assessing content depth, logical structure, and creativity.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Reviews AWE strengths and limitations; supports the proposal's distinction between surface and meaning-level feedback accuracy.

new_papers_high_value.title: Co-Writing with AI, on Human Terms: Aligning Research with User Demands Across the Writing Process
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Identified four design strategies for AI writing support (structured guidance, guided exploration, active co-writing, critical feedback) mapped across planning, translating, reviewing, and monitoring.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Design framework for AI writing tools mapped to cognitive writing processes; applicable to Streamlit tool design for Studies 3 and 4.

new_papers_high_value.title: Metaheutagogy: A Framework for AI-Mediated Metacognitive Learning
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Proposes a framework positioning AI as a reflective dialogic partner emphasizing metacognitive regulation, judgement-centred capability, and ethical technology use.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Framework for positioning AI as metacognitive partner rather than answer-giver; directly applicable to Study 4 training design.

new_papers_high_value.title: Metacognitive Engagement in AI-Supported Learning: Frameworks, Challenges, and Transformations
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Identifies how metacognitive knowledge types are activated in AI-supported learning and what kinds of feedback trigger self-regulation and reflective thinking.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Links feedback types to metacognitive activation; directly relevant to automated feedback classification (Study 2) and metacognitive outcomes (Study 4).

new_papers_high_value.title: Do Generative AI Tools Enhance Students' Learning Performance or Promote Laziness and Academic Cheating?
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Students using AI as supplemental tool performed significantly worse on unannounced quiz, indicating AI promotes laziness, dependency, and shortcut-taking behavior.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Empirical evidence for AI-induced learning dependency; strengthens the metacognitive laziness argument in the proposal.

new_papers_high_value.title: Enhancing graduate students' evaluative skills through AI-supported collaborative marking
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: AI-supported collaborative marking improved inter-rater consistency (MAE from 3.260 to 1.221) and reduced deviations from expert ratings compared to student-only or AI-only conditions.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Directly relevant to evaluative judgement development in Study 4; AI-supported marking as metacognitive scaffold for calibration.

new_papers_high_value.title: Comparing ChatGPT Feedback and Peer Feedback in Shaping Students' Evaluative Judgement of Statistical Analysis: A Case Study
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: ChatGPT provided timely detailed guidance but limited support for verifying accuracy; peer feedback promoted critical reflection but varied in quality; combining both may better support novice researchers.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Directly compares ChatGPT vs peer feedback for evaluative judgement — supports hybrid feedback design and links to evaluative judgement theory in the proposal.

new_papers_high_value.title: Assessing AI-Driven Metacognitive Offloading: Initial Development and Validation of the Metacognitive Laziness Scale
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Developed and validated a Metacognitive Laziness Scale for measuring AI-driven cognitive offloading in education.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Provides measurement instrument for metacognitive offloading — potentially useful as a pre/post measure in Study 4 intervention.

new_papers_high_value.title: The Illusion of Understanding: How Middle-Schoolers Fail to Regulate Inquiry with ChatGPT in a Science Task
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Students struggled to discriminate prompt types, failed to detect vague AI explanations, and terminated inquiry prematurely; positive AI attitudes were negatively associated with interaction quality.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Demonstrates metacognitive regulation failures with GenAI even among digital natives; supports need for explicit training on evaluating AI feedback quality.

new_papers_high_value.title: Generative AI in English Language Education: Potential, Challenges, and the Path Forward
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: GenAI offers personalized instruction and immediate feedback for English productive skills, but methodological limitations, assessment concerns, and ethical considerations require human-centered approaches.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Situates GenAI in English language education (TESOL) including writing feedback — directly relevant backdrop for the proposal's Scoping Review (Study 0).

new_papers_high_value.title: The Perception by University Students of the Use of ChatGPT in Education
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Students viewed ChatGPT favorably for saving time, personalized tutoring, feedback, and writing ideation; main barriers were inability to assess source quality and accuracy.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Student perception data on ChatGPT for writing: identifies inability to assess accuracy as key barrier — supports the evaluative judgement training rationale.

new_papers_high_value.title: AI-generated feedback in an EAP writing classroom: The collaborative process of feedback, uptake, and revision quality
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Investigates AI-generated feedback in EAP writing, focusing on the collaborative process of feedback delivery, student uptake, and revision quality.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: JEAP publication directly examining AI feedback in EAP — exactly the proposal's context; collaborative feedback process aligns with Study 1 design.

new_papers_high_value.title: Generative AI-assisted feedback and EFL writing: a study on proficiency, revision frequency and writing quality
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Investigated GenAI-assisted feedback for EFL writing; examined proficiency, revision frequency, and writing quality outcomes.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Directly relevant to feedback quality and revision analysis in L2 writing; supports Study 1's feedback accuracy mapping approach.

new_papers_high_value.title: Navigating AI feedback in translation training: how text type, proficiency, and attitude shape students' acceptance behaviors
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Students accepted 68.2% of LLM suggestions; acceptance shaped by text type, proficiency, and AI attitude; rejection often triggered deeper engagement (self-revision, external verification).
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Provides acceptance rate baseline and shows rejection triggers deeper cognitive processing — directly relevant to uptake taxonomy and engagement analysis.

new_papers_high_value.title: Beyond the monorail perspective: constructing a dual-role engagement framework in peer feedback for second language oral communication
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Proposes a dual-role engagement framework examining learners' engagement as both feedback providers and recipients in peer feedback contexts.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Framework for dual-role engagement in feedback — applicable to understanding how L2 writers engage as both feedback evaluators and recipients.

new_papers_high_value.title: A Systematic Comparative Review of Generative AI vs. Teacher Feedback in EFL Writing: Learner Perceptions, Preferences, and Psychological Factors
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: GenAI reduces anxiety and improves surface-level errors; teacher feedback fosters higher-order argumentation and metacognitive awareness; hybrid models are superior.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Systematic review directly comparing GenAI vs teacher feedback in EFL writing — strong candidate for inclusion in Scoping Review (Study 0).

new_papers_high_value.title: Deep Learning Methods to Automate Students' Writing Evaluation for Improved Writing Outcomes
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: DeBERTa-v3-large consistently outperformed other models across six analytical writing measures (cohesion, syntax, vocabulary, phraseology, grammar, conventions) for AES.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Directly validates DeBERTa-v3-large for automated writing evaluation — supports Study 2's choice of DeBERTa-v3 as classifier backbone.

new_papers_high_value.title: Explainable Automatic Grading with Neural Additive Models
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Neural Additive Model combined performance of neural networks with explainability of additive models for ASAG, using Knowledge Integration features.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Relevant to explainability requirements in the proposal's classifier design; alternative approach for interpretable automatic grading.

new_papers_high_value.title: Mind the Gap: Automated Corpus Creation for Enthymeme Detection and Reconstruction in Learner Arguments
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Created 40,089 argument instances for enthymeme detection and reconstruction using ICLEv3 corpus of learner essays; automated gap detection in argumentation.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Uses ICLEv3 (same corpus the proposal plans to use) for automated learner argument analysis — directly methodological precedent for Study 2.

new_papers_high_value.title: A hybrid deep learning and fuzzy logic framework for feature-based evaluation of English Language Learners
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Hybrid DeBERTa+Metadata+LSTM model achieved 93% accuracy for language proficiency prediction; syntax, cohesion, and vocabulary centrality confirmed by SHAP analysis.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Technical precedent for DeBERTa-based classification with explainability (SHAP) for ELL evaluation — directly informs Study 2 classifier architecture.

new_papers_high_value.title: An Experiential Approach to AI Literacy
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Proposes experiential AI literacy that integrates participants' daily experiences through storytelling and brainstorming grounded AI use cases, bridging knowing-doing gap.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Pedagogical approach for AI literacy training — experiential (not text-only) aligns with the proposal's practice-based training design for Study 4.

new_papers_high_value.title: Supporting Feedback and Trust in CBME: A Conjoint WBA Workshop for Trainers and Trainees
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Conjoint WBA workshop enhanced feedback literacy behaviours ('Provide Feedback', 'Manage Affect') in medical education setting.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Feedback literacy intervention study showing measurable improvement in feedback literacy behaviours; relevant to feedback literacy component even if medical context.

new_papers_high_value.title: Unlocking potential in underachieving learners: self-regulation development through AI-supported e-mentoring among socioeconomically disadvantaged students
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: AI-supported e-mentoring (goal clarification, micro-strategy prompting, reflective coaching) improved SRL, learning engagement, and persistence in cluster RCT.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Empirical RCT evidence for AI-supported SRL training with compensatory benefits for low-baseline students — strong precedent for Study 4 training effects.

new_papers_high_value.title: Students' perspectives of the change from human led- to AI-led feedback – a review of Studiosity's Writing Feedback
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Students value AI feedback for speed and consistency but prefer humans for nuanced understanding; hybrid approaches recommended.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Student voice on AI vs human feedback in writing; supports hybrid feedback preference and the preference-reality gap argument.

new_papers_high_value.title: Automated Evaluation of ESL Learners' English Writing Skills through AI Writing Analytics
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Reviews AWE systems including DL, NLP, and GenAI approaches for real-time feedback, linguistic feature extraction, and integration with teacher practices.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Comprehensive review of AWE approaches for ESL writing — relevant to the Scoping Review and situating the proposal's technical approach.

new_papers_high_value.title: A Systematic Comparative Review of GenAI vs Teacher Feedback in EFL Writing
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: GenAI reduces anxiety and improves surface-level errors; teacher feedback fosters higher-order argumentation; hybrid models are superior.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Systematic review directly comparing GenAI vs teacher feedback in EFL writing — ideal for inclusion in Study 0 scoping review.

new_papers_high_value.title: Systematic review of research on artificial intelligence applications in higher education – where are the educators?
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: AIEd applications cluster into profiling/prediction, assessment/evaluation, adaptive systems, and intelligent tutoring; weak connection to pedagogical theory.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Foundational AIEd systematic review; identifies the gap in pedagogical grounding that the proposal addresses.

new_papers_high_value.title: Supporting STEM students' self-regulatory skills through developing their assessment literacy and evaluative judgement capabilities
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Addresses how assessment literacy and evaluative judgement development support self-regulatory skills in STEM students.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Links assessment literacy to evaluative judgement — relevant theoretical framing for feedback literacy in the proposal.

new_papers_high_value.title: Online peer assessment in Galenic Pharmacy: enhancing evaluative judgement in higher education
new_papers_high_value.key_finding: Online peer assessment with iterative deliverables improved feedback quality across iterations and enhanced critical judgement.
new_papers_high_value.reason_valuable: Evidence for peer assessment improving evaluative judgement through iterative practice; supports the practice-based approach in Study 4.

new_papers_low_value.title: Computer and Internet Literacy Course of the College of Computer Science for Municipality of Agoo, La Union
new_papers_low_value.key_finding: Basic ICT literacy training for barangay officials in the Philippines.
new_papers_low_value.reason_valuable: Too narrow/basic; not relevant to GenAI feedback or L2 writing.

new_papers_low_value.title: Assessing Data Literacy in K-12 Education: Challenges and Opportunities
new_papers_low_value.key_finding: Data literacy assessment challenges in K-12 education.
new_papers_low_value.reason_valuable: Data literacy, not feedback literacy or AI; K-12 context, not L2 writing.

new_papers_low_value.title: Building evaluative judgement through the process of feedback
new_papers_low_value.key_finding: Evaluative judgement can be built through feedback processes.
new_papers_low_value.reason_valuable: Theoretical overlap with already-cited Tai et al. (2018); book chapter from the same Routledge volume. Low marginal value.

new_papers_low_value.title: Operationalising Dialogic Feedback to Develop Students' Evaluative Judgement and Enactment of Feedback
new_papers_low_value.key_finding: Dialogic feedback operationalization for evaluative judgement development.
new_papers_low_value.reason_valuable: Theoretical overlap with Carless & Boud already cited; book chapter, limited added value.

new_papers_low_value.title: Exemplars, feedback and bias
new_papers_low_value.key_finding: Exemplars can introduce bias in feedback processes.
new_papers_low_value.reason_valuable: Peripheral; book chapter on exemplar bias, not directly relevant to AI feedback.

new_papers_low_value.title: Limits to evaluative judgement
new_papers_low_value.key_finding: Identifies limits and boundary conditions of evaluative judgement.
new_papers_low_value.reason_valuable: Theoretical but marginal; book chapter from the same Routledge volume as already-cited sources.

new_papers_low_value.title: SWOT analysis of ChatGPT: Implications for educational practice and research
new_papers_low_value.key_finding: ChatGPT strengths include personalized responses; weaknesses include lack of deep understanding; threats include declining cognitive skills.
new_papers_low_value.reason_valuable: Useful overview but well-cited and framework-level; may already be reflected via other citations. Consider if space permits.

new_papers_low_value.title: ChatGPT for good? On opportunities and challenges of large language models for education
new_papers_low_value.key_finding: LLMs offer opportunities for personalized learning but require careful integration to address bias, reliability, and critical thinking.
new_papers_low_value.reason_valuable: Broad overview; useful as background but not specific enough to merit dedicated citation in a methods-heavy proposal.

new_papers_low_value.title: The Robots Are Here: Navigating the Generative AI Revolution in Computing Education
new_papers_low_value.key_finding: Multi-country survey of LLM use in computing education: attitudes, pedagogical changes, ethical issues.
new_papers_low_value.reason_valuable: Computing education context; not L2 writing. Low direct relevance.

new_papers_low_value.title: Unveiling Students' Experiences and Perceptions of AI Usage in Higher Education
new_papers_low_value.key_finding: Students report benefits of increased productivity and personalized learning alongside concerns about over-reliance.
new_papers_low_value.reason_valuable: Generic AI perceptions; no writing-specific focus. Low marginal value over already-cited student perception studies.

new_papers_low_value.title: Recommended "AI, Expert or Peer?" paper is already cited (Jacobsen et al. 2025 line 411)
Feedback and Automated Writing Evaluation (AWE) - Na & Duris & Hegelheimer
new_papers_low_value.key_finding: ChatGPT valued for global writing aspects (content, organization) but less effective for sentence-level issues; feedback often overly general and positive.
new_papers_low_value.reason_valuable: Useful finding about ChatGPT feedback characteristics but book chapter format limits citable authority. Low-medium value.

new_papers_low_value.title: Optimizing DeBERTa V3 for Accurate Automated Essay Evaluation in Indonesian Educational Context
new_papers_low_value.key_finding: DeBERTa V3 for AEE in Indonesian context.
new_papers_low_value.reason_valuable: Conference proceedings; no abstract available. Limited citable authority compared to journal publications.

new_papers_low_value.title: Leveraging Hybrid Intelligence: Teacher Agency in Human-AI Collaborative Writing Feedback (listed twice)
